segments.title: A 64-gun HMS Africa in song
segments.summary: A short musical salute presents HMS Africa as an enduring 64-gun Royal Navy ship amid imagined battle imagery.
segments.body: This special is a celebratory song rather than a conventional history lesson. Its refrain treats Africa as a resilient ship under fire, while the closing verse calls her an old 64-gun vessel. Open this moment for a compact commemorative portrait of how a Trafalgar-era warship can be remembered as more than a list of specifications.

evidence.note: The song repeatedly names HMS Africa, describes shell and shot falling on her, and identifies her as an old 64-gun ship.

segments.title: Continue with the Trafalgar videos
segments.summary: The closing note points viewers who want more on HMS Africa to the channel's Trafalgar video series.
segments.body: After the performance, Dr. Clarke calls HMS Africa a very cool ship and directs viewers to a link below for the Trafalgar videos. This makes the special a brief introduction and a pointer toward the longer Battle of Trafalgar material, rather than a standalone ship history.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke describes HMS Africa as a very cool ship and directs viewers to the Trafalgar videos through a link below.
